name: Joe Comfort
abstract: Joe Comfort (July 18, 1917 – October 29, 1988) was an American jazz double bassist.

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio
Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio

works.t: No No Nanette: Tea for Two

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: Me, and Joe Comfort, and James Nelson, Hawk, Lorenzo, myself on drums and Doris Lathy on guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gus Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: And my first sergeant was Joe Comfort... The bass player, he used to play with... I think we were the only one that did it in Camp Rucker. And Joe Comfort was surprised about that himself

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Comfort left to go to Lionel Hampton -- Joe Comfort left to go with Nat King Cole. And he asked me to That's when he joined the band. ... So I joined the band.

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: the bassist was Joe Comfort and the guitar player was John Collins, a very fine guitarist and a long-time friend. They made up the Nat King Cole Trio.

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: They didn’t have a bass at that time, and later when they added the bass Joe Comfort, who was a fine bassist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: Nat King Cole had called me. He wanted me to take Joe Comfort’s place. Because Joe was leaving.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Lewis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995
